select_target_if: sample values contain term/add/update/new/terminate — maps to pdmTransactionType
reject_target_if: values are MD/DO/NP/PA — that's practitionerRole; values are primary/secondary — that's locationType
example_fail: Provider Type → pdmTransactionType (values are 'MD', 'DO' — that's practitionerRole)

trigger_pattern: state code column (2-letter e.g. AL, CA, TX as header)
select_target_if: column header is exactly a 2-letter US state code AND neighboring columns also have state codes — this is a state license matrix; mark all as skip with note for melt/unpivot transform
reject_target_if: header is a state code but values are city names or long text — may be a location column
example_pass: TX (column header in a state license matrix) → skip (needs melt transform)
example_fail: TX → stateLicenseIssuingState (wrong — it's a column header, not a value)

select_target_if: practitioner roster and values look like person names (First Last) — map to firstName but flag as needs split transform
reject_target_if: facility roster or values look like organization names — map to facilityName or groupLegalBusinessName
example_pass: Full Name → firstName [needs split transform to extract firstName/lastName]
example_fail: Full Name → groupLegalBusinessName (only if values are org names)

select_target_if: column refers to languages the practitioner speaks — use practitionerLanguage (multi-value, may need split)
reject_target_if: column refers to office/staff languages (not practitioner) — still use practitionerLanguage as no separate office language field exists
example_fail: Office Languages → groupPracticeLocationLanguage (field doesn't exist in schema)

select_target_if: 2-letter code in a state license matrix context (neighboring state code columns) → skip (melt needed); if adjacent to license number column → stateLicenseIssuingState
reject_target_if: 'MD' in practitioner context with degree values like 'Doctor of Medicine' → providerType; 'SC' alone is ambiguous
example_pass: DC → stateLicenseIssuingState (when column contains 'DC' state abbreviation next to license numbers)
example_fail: MD → stateLicenseNumber (incorrect — 'MD' as column header in a degree context means Medical Doctor)

select_target_if: recred DUE/next/upcoming → nextCredentialingDate; recred last/previous/complete → lastCredentialingDate
reject_target_if: initial/first credentialing → initialCredentialingDate
example_pass: Recred Due Date → nextCredentialingDate; Last Recred Date → lastCredentialingDate
